dataset_version_target: official (Xiao et al., CVPR 2024) -- val: 567 videos / 3358 questions, test: 990 videos / 5553 questions
official_source_note: Official dataset/download location not recorded in this repo; not fetched during this setup task (no network dataset download performed, per task constraints).
status: OFFICIAL_DATASET_NOT_VERIFIED -- SETUP GATE 3 (dataset manifest complete) DOES NOT PASS AT OFFICIAL SCALE

local_assets_found.grounding_annotation_val_schema: dict keyed by video_id -> {duration (seconds), fps, location: {question_index_str: [[start_sec, end_sec], ...]}}
local_assets_found.integrity_finding_critical: gsub_val.json and gsub_test.json are BYTE-IDENTICAL (same SHA-256, same 999-video keyset). This means there is NO distinct official test-split grounding annotation file present in the repo -- 'test' is a duplicate of 'val'. This alone is disqualifying for running an official test-split evaluation and is recorded as a blocking finding in setup_failures.jsonl.
local_assets_found.video_coverage_finding: Only 91 of the videos referenced by val.csv / gsub_val.json have an actual .mp4 file under NExTVideo_flat (91 present vs 1000 referenced in val.csv, 999 in gsub_val.json). This repo checkout has never held the full NExT-GQA video corpus -- only a small locally-curated subset.

non_official_working_subset.name: nextqa_exp1a_subset (informal internal name; historically referred to as 'the 89-question subset', actually 92 videos / 89 with usable events / 255 timed events)
non_official_working_subset.multiple_gold_spans_per_question: not modeled in this CSV -- one (start_time,end_time) row per query/event; if a query recurs across rows it is a distinct annotated event, not a second gold span for the same question
non_official_working_subset.known_prior_usage: consumed by benchmark_runs/t0_nextgqa_89_cpu (an earlier, smaller exploratory run -- not an official NExT-GQA evaluation, not reused/overwritten by this setup task)
non_official_working_subset.explicit_label: THIS IS A PLACEHOLDER / DRY-RUN DATASET. It does not meet official NExT-GQA split sizes (567 val / 990 test videos; 3358 / 5553 questions). Per explicit user decision on 2026-07-20, it is registered here so that setup infrastructure (schemas, commands, split guard, sweep configs) can be authored and dry-run tested against real files without waiting on an official dataset acquisition step. No metric may be reported against this subset as if it were the official NExT-GQA result.

official_split_verification.validation_videos_found: NOT VERIFIABLE -- no dataset artifact in this repo is confirmably the official 567-video val split (gsub_val.json has 999 keys, not 567, and its provenance/authenticity relative to the official release is unverified)
official_split_verification.test_videos_found: NOT VERIFIABLE -- gsub_test.json is byte-identical to gsub_val.json, confirming it is not an independent official test annotation
official_split_verification.validation_questions_found: NOT COMPUTED -- grounding-annotation question count depends on resolving the gsub_val.json authenticity question first
official_split_verification.video_duration_source: gsub_val.json 'duration' field (seconds) where present; local mp4 sidecar '.metadata' files for the 91 locally-held videos (not cross-validated against gsub duration in this pass)
official_split_verification.verdict: STOP CONDITION MET per task Section 3 ('Stop if official dataset counts or mappings cannot be verified'). Recorded and NOT bypassed: full official-scale dataset setup is an open blocker, see setup_failures.jsonl and setup_report.md. Only the explicitly-labeled non-official 89-video subset above is registered as usable for infrastructure dry-runs.

missing_file_list: not enumerable at official scale (no manifest of official file names available locally to diff against)
duplicate_id_list: gsub_val.json and gsub_test.json are duplicate content, see integrity_finding_critical
invalid_annotation_list: not evaluated at official scale; not evaluated for the placeholder subset (would require running the metric/validation code against it, which is out of scope for this SETUP-ONLY task per instructions)
